title: CyBench Optimization
description: Improved a cybersecurity LLM benchmark, cutting token usage by ~47% via chain compression, dynamic memory, and feedback loops.
modalSubtitle: LLM Systems Engineering · Prompt Engineering · Oct – Dec 2025
overview: CyBench is a cybersecurity benchmark designed to evaluate how well large language models reason about real-world exploitation scenarios and CTF-style tasks. I improved the efficiency and accuracy of this system through three distinct engineering efforts.

sections.heading: What I Built
bullets: Chain Compression: Filtered trivial agent steps and delegated them to lightweight models, reducing total AI token usage by ~47% without sacrificing task quality.
Dynamic Memory: Implemented a persistent, JSON-backed memory system that injects cross-run execution context directly into LLM prompts, enabling the model to learn from prior attempts.
Feedback Loop: Logged failed actions as counterexamples and fed them back into subsequent runs, improving subtask completion rates and iteration efficiency.
